name: The Visual Language of Intent
description: Each image represents a distinct ontological category — the visual output of describing WHAT content is rather than HOW it should look. Six categories, six visual languages, one semantic engine.

image.name: Environment — The Geometry of Content Flow
image.description: When you write genesis-environment('distributed'), the engine produces an auto-fit CSS Grid that reflows from single column on mobile to multi-column on desktop — without a single media query. The grid is not responsive by configuration. It is responsive by nature.
image.caption: Distributed environment: auto-fit grid that breathes

image.name: Environment — The Reading Corridor
image.description: genesis-environment('focused') constrains content to 60–75 characters per line — the range where the human eye can track from line ending to line beginning without losing its place. No width values. No max-width. Just the word 'focused' and the engine produces a reading corridor.
image.caption: Focused environment: a corridor for sustained reading

image.name: Entity — The Frosted Glass Surface
image.description: genesis-entity('primary') produces a glassmorphism surface: backdrop-filter blur, a subtle brightness lift, barely-there borders, and depth shadows that float the card above the page. The surface treatment is an identity — primary, secondary, imperative — not a set of visual instructions.
image.caption: Primary entity: glassmorphism card with depth and identity

image.name: Cognition — Voices in the Type Scale
image.description: Each cognition variant is a distinct voice: axiom speaks in bold headlines, discourse reads in comfortable serif, protocol narrates in monospace precision, gloss whispers in small metadata, motive declares in uppercase authority, and quantum chips in badge-sized fragments. One mixin. One word. The voice changes.
image.caption: Cognition system: every variant is a distinct voice

image.name: Atmosphere — Where Light Meets Dark
image.description: genesis-atmosphere('void') is not dark mode. It is depth — OKLCH lightness 0.08, inset shadows, inverted text, the feeling of looking into deep water. 'Ethereal' is its opposite — frosted glass translucence at L = 0.99. A page can contain both. The atmosphere paints regions, not pages.
image.caption: Void atmosphere: depth, not merely darkness

image.name: Synapse — The Feel of Every Action
image.description: Navigate lifts with a gentle underline. Execute glows with neon confidence. Inquiry fades in with curiosity. Destructive warns with caution red. Social warmly invites. Every interaction type in the system maps to a synapse variant, each producing distinct hover, focus, and touch feedback — with guaranteed 44px targets on mobile.
image.caption: Synapse system: every action has its own tactile signature
